name: Mzansi Mix Vol. 1
description: General logic and reasoning with a South African flavour
author: Game Night Crew

questions.question: A R5 coin is round and flat. How many sides does it have?
questions.explanation: Eish, this one's easy! Any flat coin has 2 sides — heads and tails. Don't let the '5' on the coin confuse you!

questions.question: Sipho buys a packet of biltong for R50 and pays with a R100 note. How much change does he get?
questions.explanation: Shame, this one's straightforward — R100 minus R50 is R50 change. Now go enjoy that biltong!

questions.question: If today is Monday in Joburg, what day is it in Cape Town?
questions.explanation: Both cities are in the same country and time zone! It's still Monday in Cape Town. No tricks here, just now you'll overthink it.

questions.question: A minibus taxi has 4 wheels. How many wheels do 3 minibus taxis have in total?
questions.explanation: 3 taxis times 4 wheels each = 12 wheels. Lekker simple maths!

questions.question: Which is heavier: 1 kg of boerewors or 1 kg of droewors?
questions.explanation: They both weigh 1 kg! A kilogram is a kilogram whether it's boerewors or droewors. Don't let your braai instincts fool you.

questions.question: Thandi has R200. She spends half at Shoprite and then half of what's left at Checkers. How much does she have now?
questions.explanation: Half of R200 = R100 spent at Shoprite, leaving R100. Half of R100 = R50 spent at Checkers, leaving R50. Still enough for a cold drink!

questions.question: A braai starts at 14:00 and the meat takes 3 hours to cook. Friends arrive 2 hours after the braai starts. How long do the friends wait for the meat to be ready?
questions.explanation: Meat is ready at 17:00. Friends arrive at 16:00. So they wait 1 hour. That's quick for a braai, shame!

questions.question: In a queue at Pick n Pay, Nomsa is 5th from the front and 8th from the back. How many people are in the queue?
questions.explanation: Nomsa is 5th from front and 8th from back. Total = 5 + 8 - 1 = 12 (we subtract 1 because we counted Nomsa twice). Eish, that's a long queue!

questions.question: A Gautrain departs every 15 minutes. If you just missed the 09:05 train, when is the next one?
questions.explanation: If trains depart every 15 minutes and the last one left at 09:05, the next one is at 09:05 + 15 minutes = 09:20. Now now you'll catch it!

questions.question: You have three Woolworths bags. The first has apples, the second has oranges, the third has apples and oranges. ALL labels are wrong. You pick one fruit from the bag labelled 'Apples and Oranges' and it's an apple. What's actually in the bag labelled 'Oranges'?
questions.explanation: Since ALL labels are wrong: The bag labelled 'Apples and Oranges' can't have both — you pulled an apple, so it has only apples. The bag labelled 'Oranges' can't have oranges, and the apples-only bag is taken, so it must have apples and oranges. The bag labelled 'Apples' must have oranges. Lekker logic puzzle!

questions.question: Eskom implements Stage 2 load shedding, cutting power to each area for 2 hours at a time, 3 times a day. How many hours per day is your area without power?
questions.explanation: 2 hours per session times 3 sessions per day = 6 hours without power. Eish, better charge your phone now now!

questions.question: A farmer near Stellenbosch plants vines in rows. Each row has 10 vines. He has 5 rows. He adds 1 more row and removes 3 vines from the new row. How many vines does he have in total?
questions.explanation: Original: 5 rows x 10 vines = 50 vines. New row has 10 - 3 = 7 vines. Total = 50 + 7 = 57 vines. That's going to make some lekker wine!

questions.question: Three friends split a R240 dinner bill at Spur. They each add a R20 tip for the waiter. How much does each person pay in total?
questions.explanation: Bill per person: R240 / 3 = R80. Plus R20 tip each = R100 per person. Shame, at least they tipped!

questions.question: A Joburg-to-Durban road trip is 600 km. If you drive at a constant 120 km/h, how many hours does the trip take?
questions.explanation: 600 km divided by 120 km/h = 5 hours. That's without stopping for a Wimpy breakfast, obviously!

questions.question: You're at a potjie competition. Potjie A takes 4 hours to cook and needs to be ready by 18:00. Potjie B takes 5 hours and also needs to be ready by 18:00. What's the latest you can start Potjie B if you start Potjie A on time?
questions.explanation: Potjie B needs 5 hours before 18:00, so latest start is 13:00. Potjie A only needs to start by 14:00. Better get that fire going early!

questions.question: A taxi rank has taxis going to 4 different townships. Each route has 3 taxis. Each taxi holds 15 passengers. If all taxis are half full, how many passengers are at the rank?
questions.explanation: 4 routes x 3 taxis = 12 taxis total. Each holds 15 but is half full = 7.5, but since we're counting capacity at half: 12 x 15 = 180 total capacity, half of that = 90 passengers. The rank is buzzing!

questions.question: A Bafana Bafana jersey costs R800. The shop has a sale: 25% off, then an additional 10% off the reduced price. What's the final price?
questions.explanation: First discount: R800 - 25% = R800 - R200 = R600. Second discount: R600 - 10% = R600 - R60 = R540. Note it's NOT 35% off the original — the second discount applies to the already reduced price!

questions.question: At a shisa nyama, every 3rd customer gets free pap and every 5th customer gets a free drink. Out of the first 30 customers, how many get BOTH free pap and a free drink?
questions.explanation: Customers who get both are multiples of both 3 and 5, i.e., multiples of 15. In the first 30 customers: 15 and 30. That's 2 lucky people. Lekker!

questions.question: You buy 3 bunny chows at R45 each and 2 samoosas at R15 each. You pay with a R200 note. How many rands change do you get?
questions.explanation: 3 bunny chows: 3 x R45 = R135. 2 samoosas: 2 x R15 = R30. Total: R135 + R30 = R165. Change: R200 - R165 = R35. Just enough for another samoosa!

questions.question: At a Pretoria hotel, rooms are numbered 1-100. The manager puts a gold star on every door that is a multiple of 6 AND a multiple of 8. How many gold stars does he use?
questions.explanation: A number that's a multiple of both 6 and 8 is a multiple of their LCM (Least Common Multiple). LCM of 6 and 8 = 24. Multiples of 24 from 1-100: 24, 48, 72, 96 = 4 gold stars. The trick is knowing you need the LCM, not just any common multiple!

questions.question: Five friends at a braai each shake hands with every other person exactly once. How many handshakes happen in total?
questions.explanation: With 5 people, the number of handshakes is 5 x 4 / 2 = 10. We divide by 2 because each handshake involves two people. The formula is n(n-1)/2. Now braai in peace!

questions.question: A Woolworths food item's price increases by 20%, then later decreases by 20%. Compared to the original price, the final price is:
questions.explanation: Say the original price is R100. Increase by 20% = R120. Decrease by 20% of R120 = R120 - R24 = R96. That's R4 less, or 4% less than the original. The percentages don't cancel out because they're applied to different amounts. Eish, maths can be sneaky!

questions.question: You turn on a geyser at the start of load shedding. Power goes off 10 minutes later and stays off for 2.5 hours. When power returns, the geyser resumes. If the geyser needs exactly 45 minutes of actual heating time, how many minutes after the power returns will it finish?
questions.explanation: The geyser heated for 10 minutes before load shedding. It needs 45 minutes total. So after power returns, it needs 45 - 10 = 35 more minutes. The time without power doesn't count as heating time!

questions.question: A spaza shop sells chips in 3 sizes: small (R5), medium (R8), and large (R14). Lerato buys some bags and pays exactly R35. Which combination did she buy?
questions.explanation: Let's check each option: A = 2(R14) + R5 = R33 (no). B = R14 + 2(R8) + R5 = R14 + R16 + R5 = R35 (yes!). C = 3(R8) + R5 = R29 (no). D = R14 + R8 + R5 = R27 (no). Only option B adds up to R35. Spaza shop maths for the win!

questions.question: A taxi from Soweto to Joburg CBD takes 40 minutes in traffic and 25 minutes without traffic. If you need to arrive by 08:00 and there's DEFINITELY traffic if you leave before 07:30 but you can't be sure about traffic after 07:30, what's the latest you can GUARANTEE arriving on time?
questions.explanation: To guarantee arrival, you must plan for worst case. If you leave at 07:20 (before 07:30), there IS traffic, so it takes 40 minutes: 07:20 + 40 = 08:00. You arrive just on time. If you leave later, say 07:25, with traffic that's 08:05 — too late! Leaving after 07:30 might have traffic too (can't be sure), so you can't rely on the 25-minute trip. The latest guaranteed-safe departure is 07:20.

questions.question: Five Joburg office workers — Ayanda, Bongani, Cynthia, Dumisani, and Fikile — sit in a row. Ayanda sits next to Bongani. Cynthia does NOT sit next to Dumisani. Fikile sits at one end. Bongani sits in the exact middle. Who sits at the other end (not Fikile's end)?
options: Could be either Cynthia or Dumisani
questions.explanation: Bongani is in the middle (position 3). Ayanda sits next to Bongani, so Ayanda is in position 2 or 4. Fikile is at one end (position 1 or 5). Cynthia and Dumisani fill the remaining spots. Since Cynthia can NOT sit next to Dumisani, and since one of them is next to Bongani (pos 2 or 4) and the other at the far end: if Ayanda is in pos 2, Fikile at pos 1, then pos 4 and 5 are left for C and D — but they'd be adjacent (not allowed). So Fikile must be at pos 5, Ayanda at pos 4, leaving pos 1 and 2 for C and D. Cynthia can't be next to Dumisani: pos 1 and 2 are adjacent — contradiction! Try Ayanda at pos 4, Fikile at pos 1: pos 2 and 5 for C and D. They're not adjacent. Cynthia at pos 2 (next to Bongani), Dumisani at pos 5. The other end is Dumisani!

questions.question: You have R500. Woolworths has a deal: buy 2 items, get the cheaper one half price. You want a jacket (R400) and a shirt (R200). How much do you save compared to full price, and can you afford both?
options: Save R100, yes you can afford it
Save R200, yes you can afford it
Save R50, yes you can afford it
questions.explanation: The cheaper item is the R200 shirt, which is half price = R100. Total: R400 + R100 = R500. You save R100 and you can just afford it with exactly R500! Lekker deal!

questions.question: At a cricket match at Newlands, the scoreboard shows a team's runs after each over: 6, 14, 18, 26, 30, ?. What's the next number in the pattern?
questions.explanation: The differences between consecutive overs: 8, 4, 8, 4, ... The pattern alternates between +8 and +4. After 30, we add 8 = 38. The batsmen are scoring in a lekker rhythm!

questions.question: At a Durban beachfront market, a vendor makes R15 profit on each beaded necklace and R10 profit on each bracelet. She can make 4 necklaces per hour or 8 bracelets per hour. To maximise profit per hour, she should make:
questions.explanation: Necklaces: 4 per hour x R15 = R60/hr. Bracelets: 8 per hour x R10 = R80/hr. Even though each necklace has higher profit, she makes bracelets faster, earning R80/hr. Sometimes quantity beats margin!

questions.question: A load shedding schedule rotates through 4 zones. Zone 1 loses power at 06:00, Zone 2 at 08:00, Zone 3 at 10:00, Zone 4 at 12:00, then Zone 1 again at 14:00, and so on. Each outage lasts 2 hours. At 11:00, which zones currently have NO power?
questions.explanation: At 11:00: Zone 1 lost power 06:00-08:00 (back on). Zone 2 lost power 08:00-10:00 (back on). Zone 3 lost power 10:00-12:00 (currently off!). Zone 4 starts at 12:00 (still on). Only Zone 3 is without power at 11:00. Eish, hope they charged their phones!

questions.question: You're driving from Cape Town to Joburg (1,400 km). Your car uses 7 litres per 100 km. Petrol costs R25/litre. You have R2,000 for fuel. How far short of Joburg will you run out of petrol (to the nearest km)?
options: You'll make it with fuel to spare
questions.explanation: With R2,000 at R25/litre, you can buy 80 litres. At 7L/100km, 80 litres takes you (80 / 7) x 100 = 1,142.9 km. Distance short: 1,400 - 1,143 = about 257 km short. Eish, better budget better next time!

questions.question: A spaza shop owner buys 100 apples for R200 and sells them at R3 each. 15 apples go bad before he can sell them. What's his profit or loss?
questions.explanation: Cost: R200 for 100 apples. He sells 85 apples (100 - 15 bad) at R3 each = R255. Profit = R255 - R200 = R55. Even with rotten stock, the madala still makes a profit!

questions.question: A Table Mountain cableway cabin holds 65 people and makes a trip every 10 minutes (up or down). Starting at 08:00, going UP only, how many people can reach the top by 09:00 (the first cabin departs at 08:00)?
questions.explanation: From 08:00 to 09:00 is 60 minutes. Departures at 08:00, 08:10, 08:20, 08:30, 08:40, 08:50, 09:00 = 7 trips (including the one AT 09:00, since it departs by then). 7 x 65 = 455 people. That's a lot of tourists on the mountain!

questions.question: In a Lotto draw, you must pick 6 numbers from 1-52. If your 6 numbers are all even, how many even numbers between 1 and 52 (inclusive) did you NOT pick?
questions.explanation: Even numbers from 1-52: 2, 4, 6, ..., 52. That's 52/2 = 26 even numbers. You picked 6 of them. So you didn't pick 26 - 6 = 20 even numbers. Now just wait for the draw!

questions.question: Two Uber drivers in Sandton start at the same point. Driver A goes 5 km north, then 12 km east. Driver B goes 12 km south, then 5 km west. How far apart are they now (straight line)?
questions.explanation: Driver A is at position (12 km east, 5 km north). Driver B is at (-5 km west, -12 km south). The difference: East-West = 12 + 5 = 17 km. North-South = 5 + 12 = 17 km. Straight-line distance = sqrt(17^2 + 17^2) = sqrt(578) = about 24.04 km. Don't be tempted to just add 17+17 = 34 — you need Pythagoras here! Those drivers are far apart, shame.

questions.question: A farmer has a 100m x 100m field near Mpumalanga. He wants to fence it, but there's a straight river along one full side (no fence needed there). Fencing costs R150/metre. He also needs a gate (3m wide, R2,000). If the gate replaces fencing on the side opposite the river, what's the total cost?
questions.explanation: Three sides need fencing: two sides of 100m and one side of 100m (opposite river) minus the 3m gate = 97m. Total fencing: 100 + 100 + 97 = 297m. Fencing cost: 297 x R150 = R44,550. Plus gate: R2,000. Total: R44,550 + R2,000 = R46,550. Farming isn't cheap, hey!

questions.question: At a jol, each person arrives and shakes hands with everyone already there. The 1st person shakes 0 hands, the 2nd shakes 1, the 3rd shakes 2, etc. If there were 45 total handshakes, how many people came to the jol?
questions.explanation: The total handshakes = 0+1+2+...+(n-1) = n(n-1)/2. We need n(n-1)/2 = 45, so n(n-1) = 90. Testing: 10 x 9 = 90. So n = 10 people. That must have been a lekker jol!

questions.question: A tank collects rainwater in Limpopo. It's currently 1/3 full. After rain, it becomes 3/4 full, gaining 500 litres. What is the tank's total capacity in litres?
questions.explanation: The rain added 3/4 - 1/3 = 9/12 - 4/12 = 5/12 of the tank. If 5/12 of the tank = 500 litres, then the full tank = 500 x 12/5 = 1,200 litres. That's a lot of water — lekker for the garden!

questions.question: A car from Durban and a car from Joburg drive toward each other on the N3 (600 km apart). The Durban car goes 100 km/h, the Joburg car goes 80 km/h. A bird flies back and forth between them at 150 km/h from the moment they start until they meet. How far does the bird fly in total?
questions.explanation: The trick is to ignore the bird's back-and-forth! The cars approach at 100 + 80 = 180 km/h combined. Time to meet: 600 / 180 = 3.33 hours. The bird flies the entire time at 150 km/h: 150 x 3.33 = 500 km. Eish, that bird is moeg!

questions.question: You have 12 identical-looking Krugerrands, but one is fake and slightly lighter. Using a balance scale, what is the MINIMUM number of weighings guaranteed to find the fake coin?
questions.explanation: Split into 3 groups of 4. Weigh group 1 vs group 2. If they balance, the fake is in group 3; if not, it's in the lighter group. Now you have 4 suspects. Split into groups of 1, 1, and 2. Weigh the two singles — if they balance, weigh the remaining 2 to find the fake. 3 weighings guaranteed. This is a classic logic puzzle, just with Krugerrands!

questions.question: A Johannesburg property agent says: 'This plot is a perfect square with an area of 2,025 m^2. The perimeter fencing costs R200/m.' Without a calculator, what's the fencing cost?
questions.explanation: You need sqrt(2025) to find the side length. Note that 45^2 = 2,025 (since 45 x 45 = 2,025). Side = 45m. Perimeter = 4 x 45 = 180m. Cost = 180 x R200 = R36,000. The trick was computing the square root mentally: 40^2 = 1,600 and 50^2 = 2,500, so try 45!

questions.question: A stokvel has 8 members. Each month, every member contributes R500, and one member takes the full pot. After how many months will every member have received the pot exactly once AND the total contributed by each member equal the total received?
questions.explanation: Each month the pot is 8 x R500 = R4,000. Each member needs to receive R4,000 once. After 8 months, each member has contributed 8 x R500 = R4,000 and received R4,000 once. It balances perfectly after 8 months — the same as the number of members. Stokvel maths is beautiful!

questions.question: Three Eskom substations (A, B, C) are connected in a triangle. The cable between A-B is 30 km, B-C is 40 km, A-C is 50 km. A new substation D must be placed on cable A-C such that D-B is the shortest possible distance. How far is D from A?
questions.explanation: Notice 30-40-50 is a right triangle (30^2 + 40^2 = 900 + 1600 = 2500 = 50^2), with the right angle at B. The shortest distance from B to AC is the altitude from B to the hypotenuse. Using area: Area = 1/2 x 30 x 40 = 600. Also Area = 1/2 x 50 x h, so h = 24 km. The distance from A to D (foot of altitude): AD = AB^2/AC = 900/50 = 18 km. Heavy maths for an Eskom problem!

questions.question: You have a 5-litre bucket and a 3-litre bucket at a Karoo farm borehole. You need exactly 4 litres. What's the minimum number of pour/fill/empty actions needed?
questions.explanation: 1) Fill the 5L bucket. 2) Pour from 5L into 3L (5L now has 2L, 3L is full). 3) Empty the 3L bucket. 4) Pour the 2L from 5L into 3L (3L now has 2L). 5) Fill the 5L bucket again. 6) Pour from 5L into 3L until full (3L needs 1L more, so pour 1L; 5L now has 4L). Six steps! Classic Die Hard puzzle, but make it Karoo.

questions.question: At a Pick n Pay, a 750ml bottle of olive oil costs R120, and a 500ml bottle costs R90. A loyalty card gives 15% off the bigger bottle only. Which is cheaper per ml with the card, and by how much per litre?
options: They're the same price per ml
questions.explanation: 750ml with 15% off: R120 x 0.85 = R102 for 750ml. Per litre: R102 / 0.75 = R136/L. 500ml at full price: R90 / 0.5 = R180/L. The 750ml bottle is cheaper by R180 - R136 = R44 per litre. That loyalty card is doing some heavy lifting!

questions.question: Three friends buy a R300 braai pack and each pay R100. The butcher realises it should only cost R250 and gives R50 back to a friend to split. The friend keeps R20 and gives R10 back to each of the other two. Now each person paid R90 (2 people) and R80 (the sneaky friend). That's R90 + R90 + R80 = R260. The butcher has R250. That's R510 total but they only started with R300. Where's the extra R210?
options: There is no missing money — the question adds numbers that shouldn't be added together
questions.explanation: This is a classic misdirection puzzle! The R260 paid (R90+R90+R80) ALREADY INCLUDES the R20 the friend kept. The correct accounting: R250 (butcher) + R20 (sneaky friend) + R30 (returned to the three friends) = R300. You shouldn't add the R260 paid to the R250 the butcher has — the R250 is part of the R260! Eish, that's sneaky maths!

questions.question: A Sandton clock tower chimes once at 1 o'clock, twice at 2 o'clock, etc. Each chime takes 1 second, and there's a 2-second gap between chimes. How many total seconds does it take for all chimes from 1 o'clock through 12 o'clock (inclusive)?
questions.explanation: For each hour h: there are h chimes (h seconds) and (h-1) gaps of 2 seconds = 2(h-1) seconds. Total for hour h = h + 2(h-1) = 3h - 2. At 1 o'clock: 1 sec. At 2: 4 sec. At 3: 7 sec... etc. Sum from h=1 to 12: 1+4+7+10+13+16+19+22+25+28+31+34. Pair them up: (1+34)+(4+31)+(7+28)+(10+25)+(13+22)+(16+19) = 6 x 35 = 210 seconds. That's a lot of chiming, eish!

questions.question: You're at a 4-way intersection in Bloemfontein. A sign says: 'Turn left to go to the hospital. The library is opposite the hospital. The school is to the right of the library (from the library's perspective, facing the intersection).' You're facing north. Where is the school?
questions.explanation: You face north. Left = west, so hospital is west. Library is opposite hospital = east. From the library facing the intersection (facing west), the school is to the library's right = north. This requires carefully tracking perspectives and directions. Only about 5% would get this right!

questions.question: Nkosi says: 'Two days ago, I was 25. Next year, I'll turn 28.' On what date is Nkosi's birthday and what date is he saying this?
questions.explanation: Nkosi is speaking on January 1st. His birthday is December 31st. Two days ago was December 30th — he was still 25. Yesterday (Dec 31) was his birthday and he turned 26. Today (Jan 1) he is 26. This calendar year (on Dec 31) he'll turn 27. Next year (the following Dec 31) he'll turn 28. It all works because the year boundary falls between his birthday and the day he's speaking! Eish, that's a brain-bender.

questions.question: A Checkers store has 3 checkout lanes. Lane 1 processes 1 customer per minute, Lane 2 processes 2 per minute, Lane 3 processes 3 per minute. 100 customers arrive at once and split optimally across lanes. What's the minimum time (in minutes) to process everyone?
questions.explanation: For optimal splitting, all lanes should finish at the same time T. Lane 1 processes T customers, Lane 2 processes 2T, Lane 3 processes 3T. Total: T + 2T + 3T = 6T = 100. So T = 100/6 = 16.67 minutes. Lane 1 handles about 17, Lane 2 about 33, Lane 3 about 50. About 16.7 minutes total. That's lekker efficient for Checkers!

questions.question: A jeweller in the Joburg Diamond District has a gold chain of 23 links. A customer wants to rent it for 23 days, paying 1 link per day. The jeweller must be able to make change (accept links back and give different ones). What is the minimum number of links he needs to CUT to enable exact payment for any number of days from 1 to 23?
questions.explanation: Cut the 4th and 11th links. This gives pieces of: 1 (the cut link), 3, 1 (the other cut link), 6, and 12 links. With pieces of 1, 1, 3, 6, 12 you can make any number from 1-23 by combining and exchanging. For example: Day 1 = give a single link. Day 2 = give the 3-piece, get 1 back. Day 3 = give the 3-piece. Etc. Only 2 cuts needed! This is a variation of the classic chain puzzle. Only the top 1% figure out the optimal cuts!

questions.question: Three wise men from Limpopo are given hats from a bag of 3 red and 2 blue hats. Each can see the other two's hats but not their own. They're asked in order. Man 1 (sees the other two) says 'I don't know my hat colour.' Man 2 (hearing Man 1, seeing Man 3) says 'I don't know either.' What colour is Man 3's hat?
questions.explanation: If Man 1 saw 2 blue hats, he'd know his was red (only 2 blue exist). He doesn't know, so he sees at most 1 blue hat. Man 2 knows this. If Man 2 saw a blue hat on Man 3, he'd reason: 'Man 1 saw at most 1 blue, and I see blue on Man 3. If I were also blue, Man 1 would've seen 2 blue and known. So I must be red.' But Man 2 doesn't know, meaning he DOESN'T see blue on Man 3. Therefore Man 3's hat is RED. Pure logical deduction — only the sharpest 1% crack this one!

questions.question: A farmer near Stellenbosch has a 1,000-litre tank of grape juice. He removes 100 litres and replaces it with water, mixes thoroughly, then repeats this process 2 more times (3 total). How many litres of pure grape juice remain (rounded to the nearest whole number)?
questions.explanation: After each cycle, the fraction of juice remaining is multiplied by (1 - 100/1000) = 0.9. After 3 cycles: 1000 x 0.9^3 = 1000 x 0.729 = 729 litres. The key insight is that each removal takes out the same proportion of juice (since it's well mixed), so you multiply by 0.9 each time. Most people incorrectly try to subtract 100 litres of juice each time, but after the first mix, you're removing a mixture, not pure juice!

questions.question: 100 lockers at a Pretoria school are all closed. Student 1 toggles every locker. Student 2 toggles every 2nd locker. Student 3 toggles every 3rd, and so on up to Student 100. After all 100 students are done, how many lockers are open?
questions.explanation: A locker ends up open only if it's toggled an odd number of times. Locker N is toggled once for each divisor of N. Most numbers have an even number of divisors (they come in pairs: 1x12, 2x6, 3x4). Only perfect squares have an odd number of divisors (because the square root pairs with itself). Perfect squares from 1-100: 1, 4, 9, 16, 25, 36, 49, 64, 81, 100 = 10 lockers. This is a legendary maths puzzle. If you got it, you're truly in the 1% club!

questions.question: You're on a game show in Mzansi. Behind 3 doors: one has a Krugerrand, the others have nothing. You pick Door 1. The host (who knows what's behind each door) opens Door 3, showing nothing. He offers you the chance to switch to Door 2. What's the probability of winning if you switch?
questions.explanation: This is the famous Monty Hall problem! Your initial pick has a 1/3 chance of being right, meaning there's a 2/3 chance the Krugerrand is behind one of the other doors. When the host reveals an empty door, that entire 2/3 probability collapses onto the remaining unopened door. Switching gives you 2/3 odds vs staying at 1/3. Most people's intuition screams 50/50, but the maths don't lie. If you got this through pure reasoning, you're a proper 1-percenter. Lekker!
